item.title: BRAC Bank promotes Md. Shaheen Iqbal, Ahmed Rashid Joy to Additional Managing Directors
item.description: The Board of Directors of BRAC Bank has promoted Md. Shaheen Iqbal, CFA and Ahmed Rashid Joy to Additional Managing Directors (AMDs) effective from March 1, 2026, according to a press release. Md. Shaheen Iqbal now serves as Additional Managing Director & Head of Wholesale Banking. He will look af

item.title: New law to stop cheating via digital devices: Education Minister
item.description: Education Minister ANM Ehsanul Hoque Milon has announced that the government will enact new legislation to curb cheating using digital devices in public examinations. The minister reaffirmed the government’s “zero-tolerance” stance on malpractice during an exchange of views with

item.title: Trump says ‘sad to see’ US-UK relationship deteriorate over Iran strikes
item.description: US President Donald Trump criticised Prime Minister Keir Starmer for a second time this week, saying it was "sad to see" the so-called special relationship deteriorate after Britain initially held off providing support for US strikes on Iran. Starmer said Britain did not take part in the assault o

item.title: Three DIGs transferred to different positions within same rank
item.description: The government has transferred and posted three senior police officers in the public interest, according to a notification issued by the Home Ministry. Deputy Inspector General (DIG) of police (Barishal Range) Md Manjur Morshed Alam has been transferred and posted as DIG of the Tourist Police. Md

item.title: Al-Arafah Islami Bank initiates 10-day iftar distribution program
item.description: Al-Arafah Islami Bank, one of the country’s leading Shariah-based banks, has initiated a 10-day Iftar distribution program for fasting people on the streets to mark the holy month of Ramadan, according to a press release. On Sunday, March 1, 2026, the bank’s Managing Director and CEO,

item.title: AB Bank hosts Iftar Mahfil to honour media personnel
item.description: AB Bank PLC has recently hosted an Iftar Mahfil to honour the media personnel of the country, according to a press release. The event was organised for the 10th time to recognise the courageous and objective role of the journalists. Mr Reazul Islam, Managing Director and CEO (Acting) delivered a

item.title: Health, arts education will be mandatory
item.description: Education and Primary and Mass Education Minister A NMEhsanul Hoque Milon has said physical and mental health as well as arts education will become compulsory for all students. He made the remarks on Tuesday at an exchange meeting with officials of the National Curriculum and Textbook Board (NCTB)

item.title: US assistant secretary of state Paul Kapur arrives
item.description: A senior US state department official is set to arrive in Dhaka on Tuesday for talks aimed at strengthening strategic relations between the United States and Bangladesh, diplomatic sources have said. S. Paul Kapur, the US assistant secretary of state for South and Central Asian affairs, will begin

item.title: Govt appoints secretaries to four ministries, divisions
item.description: The government has appointed new secretaries to four key ministries and divisions as part of a major reshuffle in the country’s administrative structure. Separate notifications in this regard were issued by the Ministry of Public Administration on Tuesday night, UNB reports. As per th

item.title: Thinking through a crisis
item.description: In fast-moving crises, early signals create strong impressions but weak understanding. For countries like Bangladesh, which feel wars mainly through prices and trade rather than battlefields, misreading the timing can be costlier than the shock itself. The following rules help read events while the

item.title: Creating jobs and driving growth-where should the focus be?
item.description: Despite notable improvements in macroeconomic indicators in recent years, Bangladesh now faces an urgent challenge: creating sufficient jobs and stimulating economic growth. The slowdown in new investment, combined with the closure of existing factories, has left many workers unemployed and weakene

item.title: School dropout and the cost to economy
item.description: That school dropout is a major hurdle undermining the foundations of education has long been recognised. Yet, when translated into economic terms, the loss assumes an even graver significance. A recent report by the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organisation (UNESCO) has quant
